learning_outcomes:
FAAS1. Major programme:Upon completion of the programme, students will:1. have gained an understanding of the development of Chinese art and
Western art (K/V);2. have acquired the ability to approach and appreciate art in a
cultural perspective (K/S/V);3. be informed of the multi-cultural environment pertaining to the
development of art in Hong Kong (K/S/V);4. have acquired the ability to demonstrate artistic skills in a
variety of media (K/S);5. have acquired the ability to apply both traditional and modern art
concepts in their artistic works (K/S);6. be academically prepared for either a professional career or
further studies (K/S/V); and7. be
able to embrace art as a way of life (V). K = Knowledge outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and
attitudes outcomes 2. Minor programme:i. Fine ArtsUpon the completion of the Minor programme, students will have
demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental issues related to the subject,
acquired knowledge of some aspects of the subject with sufficient exposure and
at a suitable level to allow further study in this subject or related subjects
or to pursue a related career. ii. Curating and Artistic StrategiesUpon completion of the Minor programme,
students will have acquired the ability to: I. Knowledge outcomes:· Demonstrate an understanding of
the development of contemporary art internationally and locally from mid-20th
to early 21st Century and the creative strategies adopted by artists· Explain key historical and
contemporary examples of exhibition design andcurating· Explain the key roles of being a curator· Demonstrate analytical skills
concerning exhibition experiences, audience engagement and sociability II Skills outcomes:· Develop skills in the various
stages of exhibition-making from a broad spectrum of real world experience and
professional networking opportunities· Demonstrate skills in idea
conceptualization, proposal writing, pitching, budgeting, contracting,
publication, exhibition design, installation and exhibition maintenance· Demonstrate artistic skills in
photographic, textual and image productions III Attitude outcomes:· Acquire personal and socialcompetencies to transfercurating into knowledgesharing and act as an active agent in facilitating knowledge engagement· Acquire personal and social
competencies to work cooperatively in a team· Develop an attitude of innovation
and creativity by challenging assumptions in the ever expanding fields of
artistic production and curation· Develop cross-disciplinary work
attitude between arts management and creative art practice
study_scheme:
Fine Arts Fine Arts Applicable to students admitted in 2023-24
Major
Programme Requirement Studies
in Fine Arts are divided into THREE main streams: (1) Art History – studies the historical development of art with a
special emphasis on China; (2) Modern Art Studio – trains students in the practice of
artistic creation; and (3) Art History and Art Studio – applies equal emphasis on both. Students must declare one of the
streams at the beginning of their third year of attendance. Students
are required to complete a minimum of 72 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
FAAS1900 and any two courses from: ANTH1020, ARTS1004, 1005, 1010, BMBL1001, CHES1100, CHLL1900
or 1902 or 1903, CURE1000, 1110, 1400 [CUMT1000], ENGE1000, HIST1000, 1700
or 1701, JASP1090, LING1000, MUSC1000, PHIL1110, THEO1000, TRAN1000
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  21
Art
History Stream[a] FAAS1100,
1110, 1130, 1140, 1600, 1700 Capstone
courses: FAAS4180, 4182
Modern
Art Studio Stream[b] FAAS1100, 1110, 1130, 1140, 1600,
1700 Capstone
courses: FAAS4280, 4282
Art
History and Art Studio Stream[c] FAAS1100, 1110, 1130, 1140, 1600,
1700 Capstone courses: [FAAS4180 and 4182] or [FAAS4280
and 4282]

Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  Students may select up to four
courses (12 units) out of CURE2031, 2037, 3017#, 3020# offered by the
Department of Cultural and Religious Studies as Art History elective
courses. Students taking the above CURE courses
in fulfilling the Fine Arts Major requirement should indicate which
course(s) is/are to be counted at their final term of attendance.
2.  |  FAAS courses at 3000 and above level as well as those labeled
as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
3.  |  A Fine Arts Major student who
pursues a second Major in Chinese Language and Literature can be exempted
from taking CHLL4403, 4332, 4121, 4401 and 4402 by taking FAAS2117, 3105,
3223, 4106 and 4109 respectively. Units earned can be counted towards
both Major Programmes.
[a]  |  A student in Art History stream is
required to submit a thesis, pass an oral examination on his/her thesis,
and participate in the Graduation Exhibition in the final year of
attendance.
[b]  |  A student in Modern Art Studio
stream is required to submit studio works for the Graduation Exhibition
and pass an oral examination in the final year of attendance.
[c]  |  A student in the Art History and
Art Studio stream is required to submit either a thesis OR studio works,
pass an oral examination, and participate in the Graduation Exhibition in
the final year of attendance.
